title: Maxillaria luteola
taxonomy: Maxillaria luteola is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Maxillaria and the subtribe Maxillariinae. Maxillaria luteola is further classified within the tribe Cymbidieae, and its family classification is under the order Asparagales. This orchid species is native to the tropical regions of Central America. As part of its taxonomic classification, Maxillaria luteola is also recognized at the kingdom level, belonging to the kingdom Plantae.

preparations: Maxillaria luteola is a species of orchid that has been used in traditional herbal medicine. intro: Maxillaria luteola, also known as the yellow maxillaria, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Maxillaria luteola include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Maxillaria luteola include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory problems.
